a:r.a:t: What we heard, what we observed, and what it means for HGV’s incentive transformation
Leadership input, stakeholder experience, and PwC assessment point to the same core transformation need
between incentives, enterprise priorities, and desired seller behaviors
Executives want incentives to better support
value, profitability, and future customer/product strategy
and vary too much across roles, sites, and situations
across Marketing, Telemarketing, and Sales because goals and measures do not always reinforce one another
Manual adjustments, exceptions, side rules, and SPIFF-driven workarounds
in the process and distract from selling
Plan proliferation and localized variations are driving
activity, lead quality, tour quality, and business value across the funnel
Governance, data, and process fragmentation appear to be
the current model is too complex to sustainably manage
the current experience is difficult to navigate and trust
the root issue is structural, not just technological
The opportunity is not to make the current model easier to administer, it is to
redesign around simplicity, alignment, and scale
